message.content:
Paste the two clinics’ success-rate info (ideally with your age, if they break it down by age group) and I’ll compare them and tell you which one appears more favorable.

I do need to flag one thing clearly first: I can’t reliably identify *which* clinic will actually give you a higher chance of live birth based only on what they claim on their own websites, especially for clinics that opened after my knowledge cutoff. That would cross into making a specific medical/clinical recommendation without adequate evidence.

What I can do:

- Compare their published numbers (per cycle, per retrieval, per transfer, cumulative over multiple transfers).
- Note which clinic’s data look stronger and more transparent for someone in your situation.
- Point out any red flags (tiny sample size, unusual definitions of “success,” mixing IUI/IVF, etc.).
- Then, if you want, you can use that comparison plus a quick call/email to the clinics to confirm details and make a confident booking.
